steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: cores now number in the dozens, hundreds, or even thousands is an independent clause. After the blank: that the once-revolutionary dual-core technology...can be found in everyday items is a dependent clause expressing the consequence of the preceding statement.

steps.title: Apply the correct punctuation rule
steps.content:
An em-dash correctly introduces a dependent clause expressing a consequence or result, and option A (thousands—that) properly applies this rule.

Options B, C, and D each fail—option B with a misplaced em-dash after that, option C with a period that creates a sentence fragment, and option D with a comma too weak to introduce a consequence clause.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
